---
title: "UIAO_114 HA / Fault Tolerance — Kickoff Plan"
subtitle: "Defer-exit criteria, baseline measurement infrastructure, and Phase 2 sequencing"
date: "2026-05-10"
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
description: "Project-plan companion to the §4.4 assessment that deferred UIAO_114. Defines the concrete criteria for promoting UIAO_114 from ⚠️ aspirational / deferred to 🟡 implementation-active, the baseline measurement infrastructure required first, and the implementation sequencing once the criteria are met."
---

## Plan metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Program | UIAO_127 (Project Plans) |
| Template | UIAO_127 Project Plan (kickoff shape) |
| Subject | UIAO_114 — High-Availability & Fault-Tolerance Layer |
| Plan version | 1.0 (first delivery) |
| Closes | Action item 114.2 from the [§4.4 assessment](2026-04-26-ha-perf-recovery-tenancy-assessment.qmd) (re-open during Phase 2) |
| Predecessors | [UIAO_117 Phase 1 recovery](2026-04-26-uiao_117-phase1-recovery.qmd) (manual SOP for Class A/B failures) |

## Purpose

The §4.4 assessment correctly deferred UIAO_114 to Phase 2. This plan
makes that deferral **operational** by naming three things explicitly:

1. **Defer-exit criteria** — the empirical signals that say "the
   substrate is now far enough along that HA work is the right next
   investment."
2. **Baseline measurement infrastructure** — what must ship *before*
   the implementation work itself, so the HA targets can be measured
   and the impl can be reviewed against the spec's SLOs.
3. **Phase 2 sequencing** — the implementation order once the
   criteria fire, so the work is not a forklift.

This is a kickoff plan, not an implementation plan. No HA code lands
in this PR; the work this plan describes lives behind the criteria
below.

## Defer-exit criteria

UIAO_114 leaves "deferred" status when **all three** of the following
fire. Until then, the substrate-status table continues to show
UIAO_114 as ⚠️ aspirational with a "deferred to Phase 2" annotation.

### C1 — A regulated tenant is on the substrate

The substrate today operates outside any FedRAMP authorization
boundary (per substrate-status §"Test-tier status"). HA is operational
infrastructure for a tenant; without a tenant, there's nothing to
keep available. C1 fires when at least one regulated agency tenant is
running production workloads on the substrate.

**Measurement:** presence of an entry in `src/uiao/canon/tenant-registry.yaml`
(does not yet exist; UIAO_119 work introduces it) with
`environment: production` and `boundary: gcc-moderate` (or named
exception per ADR-059).

### C2 — A real evidence freshness baseline exists

The spec's SLOs (M365/AAD evidence <15min lag, drift <5min, OSCAL
<10min) are aspirational without an empirical baseline. C2 fires when
the orchestrator has logged 30 consecutive days of pipeline timing
against the production tenant from C1.

**Measurement:** `EvidenceFreshnessReport` (see baseline infrastructure
below) emits 30 days of structured per-pipeline timing without gaps.

### C3 — A platform team is named the owner

HA is operational-infrastructure work, not core governance. The
substrate maintainer (singular today) cannot honor the HA SLOs
themselves. C3 fires when a platform team is formally assigned
ownership of the substrate's runtime environment.

**Measurement:** ADR landed naming the platform team and their
escalation contract; entry in `OWNERS` file at the substrate root.

## Baseline measurement infrastructure (ships first)

Before any HA implementation work begins, the substrate must be able
to *measure* its own availability and freshness against the spec's
SLOs. Three deliverables, in order:

### B1 — Per-pipeline timing surface (extends UIAO_115 work)

The orchestrator already records wall-clock duration per plane and
per adapter run (per the §4.4 UIAO_115 assessment). B1 extends this
into a structured `PipelineTimingReport` API that:

- emits per-run, per-plane latency in canonical claim form
- ages out into the data-lake archive on the same retention contract
  as evidence (UIAO_109)
- is queryable via the Auditor API with a 90-day rolling window

This is foundational for both UIAO_114 (availability measurement)
and UIAO_115 (latency-budget enforcement). B1 is shared infrastructure;
the [UIAO_115 kickoff plan](2026-05-10-uiao_115-perf-kickoff-plan.qmd)
re-uses it.

### B2 — Regional health probe

A periodic `RegionalHealthCheck` that records:

- Substrate API reachability per declared region
- Evidence freshness per tenant per pipeline (drift staleness >5min,
  OSCAL staleness >10min)
- Adapter dispatch success rate per adapter per region

Emitted into the same canonical claim chain as B1. Surfaces the
"is the region degraded?" signal that any failover orchestration would
have to consume.

### B3 — Failover-event provenance schema

Per spec §6, failover/failback events must be cryptographically bound
to provenance. B3 ships the canonical claim schema for failover
events under `src/uiao/schemas/failover-event/` plus the
`FailoverEventEmitter` library that signs events and writes them
into the evidence chain.

B3 is **schema + library only** — no runtime that triggers failover.
That work is the implementation phase below.

## Implementation sequencing (after defer-exit criteria fire)

Once C1–C3 have fired and B1–B3 have shipped, implementation
proceeds in this order. None of these blocks is large, but each
gates the next.

### Phase 2A — Single-region HA hardening (~2 weeks)

- `RetryPolicy` exposed as canon-configured per-adapter (replaces
  the hardcoded 0.5s base / 2-attempt loop in
  `src/uiao/orchestrator/scheduler.py`).
- Adapter circuit-breakers with health-check-gated half-open state.
- Plane-level error-budget tracking emitting structured budget claims.

Output: substrate-status table marks UIAO_114 as 🟡 (single-region HA
shipped). The cross-region work below extends from there.

### Phase 2B — Multi-region foundation (~3 weeks)

- Cross-region data-lake replication via the platform team's chosen
  storage primitive (out of scope for substrate code; substrate
  consumes whatever the platform team provides).
- `RegionalCohort` declaration in tenant registry; tenant home/backup
  region binding.
- Standby-region instance of the FastAPI service in
  `src/uiao/api/`, read-only against replicated lake.

### Phase 2C — Failover orchestration (~3 weeks)

- `FailoverController` consumes B2 health probes; trigger criteria
  declared in canon (not hardcoded).
- Promotion workflow: freeze-writes → promote-metadata → promote-
  services → resume-pipelines → emit signed failover claim per B3.
- Failback workflow: reverse, with re-replication validation.

### Phase 2D — SLO enforcement and error-budget consumption

- Latency-budget alerts wired to the platform team's paging system.
- Error-budget consumption emitted into POA&M when exhausted.
- Annual SLO review cycle declared in canon.

## What stays out of scope (for this kickoff)

- **The platform-team selection itself.** That is an organizational
  decision the substrate cannot make.
- **Specific cloud-provider or storage-replication choices.** The
  substrate code consumes whatever the platform team provides; it
  does not encode AWS/Azure/GCP-specific replication primitives.
- **HA at the canon authority level.** Canon ships inside the
  installable Python package via `importlib.resources`; canon
  availability is a function of how the package is distributed, not
  of substrate code. That is a separate concern (release engineering,
  UIAO_118).

## Action items

| # | Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| 114.K1 | Land this plan; substrate-status §"Open backlog" links to it under Tier 2 item 5 | Substrate maintainer | This PR |
| 114.K2 | C1 (regulated tenant) — track via UIAO_119 tenant-registry work | UIAO_119 owner | Phase 2 kickoff |
| 114.K3 | B1 (per-pipeline timing surface) — design + ship | Substrate maintainer + UIAO_115 owner | Before C2 fires |
| 114.K4 | B2 (regional health probe) — design + ship | Platform team (once named) | After C3 fires |
| 114.K5 | B3 (failover-event schema + emitter) | Substrate maintainer | Before Phase 2A |
| 114.K6 | C3 (platform team ownership) — author the ADR | Substrate maintainer + sponsor | Before Phase 2A |

## Status reporting contract

Until the kickoff fires, substrate-status §"Open backlog" Tier 2 item
5 is the canonical status surface. The annotation in that item should
read:

> **UIAO_114 HA / Fault Tolerance** — deferred to Phase 2 per the
> 2026-04-26 §4.4 assessment. Defer-exit criteria and baseline
> measurement infrastructure documented in the
> [2026-05-10 kickoff plan](2026-05-10-uiao_114-ha-kickoff-plan.qmd).
> No implementation work in flight.

Once C1–C3 fire and B1–B3 ship, the row updates to "🟡 working" with
links to the per-phase project plans (Phase 2A/B/C/D each ships its
own UIAO_127 plan instance).

## Related documents

- [§4.4 Assessment — UIAO_114 / 115 / 117 / 119](2026-04-26-ha-perf-recovery-tenancy-assessment.qmd)
- [UIAO_117 Phase 1 Recovery](2026-04-26-uiao_117-phase1-recovery.qmd)
- [UIAO_115 Performance Kickoff Plan](2026-05-10-uiao_115-perf-kickoff-plan.qmd)
- Canon source: [`src/uiao/canon/specs/ha.md`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/ha.md)
